atiS.title: ATI-S (Affinity for Technology Interaction - Short)
atiS.item1: I like to occupy myself in greater detail with technical systems.
atiS.item2: I like testing the functions of new technical systems.
atiS.item3: It is enough for me that a technical system works, I don't care how or why.
atiS.item4: It is enough for me to know the basic functions of a technical system.

pttA.title: PTT-A (Propensity to Trust AI Systems - Abbreviated)
pttA.item1: Even if I sometimes have to bear the consequences of trusting AI systems, I still prefer to trust them rather than not trust them.
pttA.item2: I feel good when I trust AI systems.
pttA.item3: I believe that I am generally better off not trusting AI systems than trusting them.
pttA.item4: I rarely trust AI systems because I cannot cope with the uncertainty.

kiExperience.title: AI Experience
kiExperience.item1: I can use AI applications in everyday life.
kiExperience.item2: I know the most important concepts related to artificial intelligence.
kiExperience.item3: I can recognize whether I am dealing with an application based on artificial intelligence.
kiExperience.item4: I can assess the consequences of AI use for society.

heroTitle.prefix: Do you trust AI with your

landing.heroSubtitle: Together, we use BenGER (Benchmark for German Law) to investigate how much trust is actually justified. To that end, we are building a comprehensive evaluation framework for LLMs in German law. Join us!

trust.sectionDescription: Our platform combines academic excellence with practical applicability for trustworthy AI evaluations in the legal domain.
trust.academicExcellenceDescription: Developed at TUM with rigorous scientific standards. All evaluation methods are peer-reviewed and recognized in the field.
trust.fullTransparencyDescription: Open source approach with publicly accessible algorithms. Traceable evaluation criteria without hidden "Black Box" processes.
trust.legalExpertiseDescription: Specifically developed for German law by legal experts. Takes into account the complexity and nuances of the German legal system.

whatIsIt.description: BenGER is an open-source web platform that integrates task creation, collaborative annotation, configurable LLM runs, and evaluation with lexical, semantic, factual, and judge-based metrics into a single end-to-end workflow. It is designed so that domain experts can operate the full benchmarking pipeline without scripting.

annotation.title: Collaborative Annotation
annotation.description: A collaborative web interface supporting multiple task formats: free-text reasoning, multiple choice, and span annotation. Quality monitoring with progress tracking and agreement indicators enables project leads to build reliable human baselines systematically.

generation.title: Configurable LLM Execution
generation.description: Batch-execute arbitrary LLMs on your tasks with configurable API keys per user or per project. Supports all major model providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and open-source models via a scalable background execution pipeline.

evaluation.title: Standardized Evaluation
evaluation.description: Evaluate results using a broad set of metrics: lexical, semantic, factual, classification, and LLM-as-a-judge. All metric configurations are stored as explicit, auditable artifacts for transparent reporting and reproducibility.

collaboration.title: Multi-Organization Collaboration
collaboration.description: Built for collaborative research across universities, public institutions, and NGOs. Tenant isolation and role-based access control enable fine-grained sharing without cross-organization data leakage.

feedback.title: Formative Feedback
feedback.description: Annotators can optionally receive LLM-based, reference-grounded feedback highlighting missing reasoning steps and common pitfalls -- improving learning value and annotation quality while keeping expert governance in control.

openSource.title: Open Source & Extensible
openSource.description: Released as free open-source software, deployable locally or institutionally. The metric and model integration layers are designed for incremental extension -- new tasks, providers, or scoring methods can be added without rewriting pipelines.

howItWorks.description: BenGER explicitly models the full legal benchmarking workflow from task definition through evaluation and publication.

create.title: Define Tasks
create.description: Legal experts define tasks and reference solutions directly in the platform. Supports free-text reasoning, multiple choice, and span annotation formats.

import.title: Import Data
import.description: Upload legal documents and configure annotation schemas. Organization-aware data separation ensures materials stay under institutional control.

annotate.title: Annotate
annotate.description: Human annotators submit solutions via the collaborative web interface with quality signals. Optional reference-grounded feedback provides constructive guidance during annotation.

generate.title: Execute Models
generate.description: Run selected LLMs on the same tasks using configurable API credentials. Batch execution across multiple models with full configuration tracking.

evaluate.title: Evaluate
evaluate.description: Results are evaluated using lexical, semantic, factual, classification, and LLM-as-a-judge metrics. Pre-defined metric configurations ensure consistency across evaluations.

report.title: Analyze & Export
report.description: Analyze results within the platform or export them for publication. Task definitions, model configurations, and metrics are stored as reusable, auditable artifacts.

whyNeeded.description: Legal AI benchmarking today is fragmented across platforms and scripts, limiting transparency, reproducibility, and participation by non-technical legal experts.

gaps.title: Closing the Tooling Gap
gaps.description: Existing solutions cover single steps -- either annotation or evaluation -- but do not provide an integrated, role-aware workflow. BenGER combines annotation, model execution, and standardized evaluation in one system, eliminating avoidable handoffs.

rigor.title: Scientific Rigor
rigor.description: In many projects, evaluation is implemented as ad-hoc scripts that are hard to reuse. BenGER externalizes tasks, model configurations, and metrics into explicit artifacts that can be audited, compared, and reused across research groups.

reproducibility.title: Reproducibility
reproducibility.description: BenGER stores task definitions, reference solutions, model configurations, and chosen metrics as shareable artifacts. This supports transparent reporting and makes it straightforward to reproduce results across groups and over time.

accessibility.title: Accessible to Domain Experts
accessibility.description: By providing a browser-based workflow for task creation, annotation, and evaluation, BenGER enables legal experts and public institutions to contribute tasks and analyze model performance without handing off materials to external engineers.

community.title: Community & Collaboration
community.description: As open-source software, BenGER lowers barriers for universities, public authorities, NGOs, and individual legal experts to participate in collaborative research on LLM capabilities in law.

news.title: Latest News & Publications
news.subtitle: Stay up to date with our latest research, publications, and project news.

items.title: BenGER Benchathon 2026
items.description: The first BenGER Benchathon takes place March 14–15, 2026 in partnership with the Legal Tech Association. Students and legal professionals complete tasks to earn points on a leaderboard.

items.title: BenGER Platform Update and Roadmap
items.description: We have completed the BenGER platform, enabling technical laypeople — typically professional jurists — to quickly create their own benchmarks from their own documents to evaluate AI tool performance.

items.title: First glimpse into BenGER — Poster Tübingen AI & Law
items.description: BenGER was presented as a poster at the Artificial Intelligence & Law Conference in Tübingen. The platform will be released as free software following conference acceptance.

howTo.title: How-To Guide
howTo.subtitle: Guides for using the BenGER benchmarking platform

platformOverview.title: Platform Overview
platformOverview.description: BenGER is a research-grade benchmarking platform for Large Language Models in the German legal domain. It provides tools for data annotation, LLM generation, and systematic evaluation of model outputs.

workflow.title: Core Workflow
workflow.description: A typical BenGER workflow follows these steps:

step1.title: Create a Project
step1.description: Set up a project with a name, data, and labeling configuration.

step2.title: Import Data
step2.description: Upload your dataset in JSON, CSV, TSV, or plain text format.

step3.title: Annotate
step3.description: Human annotators label the data using the configured annotation interface.

step4.title: Generate
step4.description: Run LLM generation across your tasks with one or more models.

step5.title: Evaluate
step5.description: Compare LLM outputs against human annotations using automated metrics.

step6.title: Report
step6.description: Publish results as a report for academic review.

navigation.title: Navigation
navigation.description: The sidebar is organized into three groups:

quickStart.title: Quick Start
quickStart.description: Dashboard (overview and stats), Reports (published project results), and Leaderboards (model comparisons).

projectsAndData.title: Projects & Data
projectsAndData.description: Projects (create and manage projects), Data Management (browse imported data), Generation (run LLM generations), and Evaluation (run and view evaluations).

knowledge.title: Knowledge
knowledge.description: How-To (this guide), Models (available LLM models), Architecture (system documentation), and About.

creating.title: Creating a Project
creating.description: Projects are created using a 3-step wizard. Navigate to Projects in the sidebar and click "Create Project".

step1.title: 1. Project Info
step1.description: Enter a project name (required) and an optional description.

step2.title: 2. Data Import (optional)
step2.description: Upload a file (JSON, CSV, TSV, TXT) via drag-and-drop, or paste text directly. You can also skip this step and import data later.

step3.title: 3. Labeling Setup
step3.description: Choose a pre-built template or write custom Label Studio XML. Templates include: Question Answering, Multiple Choice, Span Annotation, and Custom.

qa.title: Question Answering
qa.description: Text display with a free-text answer field. Suitable for open-ended questions.

multipleChoice.title: Multiple Choice
multipleChoice.description: Text display with selectable options and an optional reasoning field.

spanAnnotation.title: Span Annotation
spanAnnotation.description: Highlight and label text spans within documents.

projectPage.title: The Project Page
projectPage.description: Each project page shows an overview of tasks, annotations, and generation/evaluation status. From here you can annotate tasks, run generations, configure evaluations, and manage project settings.

formats.title: Supported Formats
formats.description: BenGER supports the following import formats:

json.title: JSON
json.description: Arrays of objects or nested structures. Each object becomes one task.

csv.title: CSV
csv.description: Comma-separated values. The first row is used as column headers.

tsv.title: TSV
tsv.description: Tab-separated values. Same as CSV but with tab delimiters.

txt.title: Plain Text
txt.description: Each line becomes a separate task.

methods.title: Import Methods
methods.upload: File Upload: Drag and drop files or use the file picker.
methods.paste: Paste: Paste text directly into the import field. The format is auto-detected.

structure.title: Data Structure
structure.description: Each imported row becomes a "task" in BenGER. Task fields are accessible as key-value pairs in the annotation interface and can be referenced in generation prompts using the $field_name syntax.

interface.title: The Labeling Interface
interface.description: Open a project and click "Annotate" to enter the labeling interface. The interface renders dynamically based on your project's Label Studio configuration.
interface.navigation: Use the Previous/Next buttons to move between tasks. Your current position is shown (e.g. "Task 3 of 50").
interface.timer: An annotation timer automatically tracks how long you spend on each task.
interface.skip: Use the Skip button to skip a task you cannot annotate. You can optionally add a comment explaining why.

labelConfig.title: Label Studio Configuration
labelConfig.description: BenGER uses Label Studio XML format to define the annotation interface. This determines what annotators see and how they interact with the data.

tags.title: Common Tags
tags.text: Text - Displays a text field from the task data
tags.choices: Choices - Radio buttons or checkboxes for classification
tags.labels: Labels - Span annotation labels for highlighting text

apiKeys.title: Setting Up API Keys
apiKeys.description: Before running generations, you must add your LLM provider API keys in your Profile settings. Navigate to your profile (top-right menu) and enter keys for the providers you want to use.
apiKeys.note: API keys are encrypted and stored per-user. Each team member manages their own keys.

running.title: Running a Generation
running.description: From a project page, open the Generation tab and configure:
running.step1: Select one or more LLM models from your configured providers
running.step3: Choose a prompt structure (if your project has multiple)
running.step4: Select generation mode: "Generate Missing Only" (skip already-generated tasks) or "Generate All" (overwrite existing)
running.step5: Click "Start Generation" to queue the tasks

promptStructure.title: Prompt Structure Configuration
promptStructure.description: The prompt structure defines how your task data is mapped into prompts for the LLM. It consists of a system prompt and an instruction prompt with field mappings.

basicFormat.title: Basic Structure Format
basicFormat.description: A generation structure consists of two components: system prompt and instruction prompt. The instruction prompt uses a template with field mappings to extract data from tasks.

fieldMapping.title: Field Mapping Syntax
fieldMapping.description: The fields object maps placeholder names to data field paths:

placeholder.label: Placeholder
placeholder.description: The name used in {{placeholders}} - can be any name you choose

fieldPath.label: Field Path
fieldPath.description: Starts with $ followed by the actual field name in your task data

fieldMapping.nested: Supports nested fields using dot notation: $parent.child

completeExample.title: Complete Example
completeExample.description: For a legal case analysis project with task data containing prompt_clean and fall fields:
completeExample.explanation: This extracts three fields from your task data and combines them into a complete prompt for the LLM.

mistake1.label: Incorrect: Using simple string format
mistake1.explanation: The instruction_prompt must be an object with template and fields properties.

mistake2.label: Incorrect: Missing $ prefix in field paths
mistake2.description: Field paths must start with $ to reference task data fields.

correct.label: Correct: Proper template with field mapping
correct.description: Use the structured format with template and fields.

bestPractices.title: Best Practices
bestPractices.practice1: Use descriptive placeholder names that match your domain
bestPractices.practice2: Test your generation structure with a single task first
bestPractices.practice3: Verify the actual field names in your task data before mapping
bestPractices.practice4: Use the template to add context, instructions, and formatting
bestPractices.practice5: Keep sensitive fields like solutions/answers out of prompts

reasoning.title: Reasoning Models
reasoning.description: BenGER has special support for reasoning/thinking models. These models have additional parameters:
reasoning.openai: OpenAI o-series: reasoning_effort setting (low/medium/high). Temperature is fixed at 1.0.
reasoning.anthropic: Anthropic Claude: thinking_budget in tokens (1024-128000) with presets.
reasoning.google: Google Gemini: thinking_budget in tokens (0-24576) with presets.
reasoning.qwen: Qwen: thinking_budget in tokens (1024-32000) with presets.

monitoring.title: Monitoring Progress
monitoring.description: Once a generation is started, you can monitor progress in real-time. The task list shows per-model status (pending, running, completed, failed) for each task. Progress updates arrive via WebSocket for instant feedback.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Evaluation compares LLM-generated outputs against human annotations (ground truth) using automated metrics. BenGER supports multi-field evaluation where you can map multiple reference fields to prediction fields.

automated.title: Automated
automated.description: Traditional NLP metrics (BLEU, ROUGE, exact match, etc.) computed automatically.

llmJudge.title: LLM-Judge
llmJudge.description: Uses an LLM to evaluate the quality of generated outputs.

human.title: Human
human.description: Human annotations serve as the ground truth for evaluation.

configuring.title: Configuring Evaluations
configuring.description: In the project page, go to the Evaluation section to configure:
configuring.step1: Select field pairs: map a reference field (human annotation) to a prediction field (LLM output)
configuring.step2: Choose metrics appropriate for each field pair (text metrics for text, exact match for classifications, etc.)
configuring.step3: BenGER auto-detects answer types from your Label Studio config and recommends suitable metrics

results.title: Viewing Results
results.description: Evaluation results are available in multiple views:
results.table: Table View: Model x Metric score matrix
results.chart: Chart View: Visual comparison across models
results.statistics: Statistics: Confidence intervals, t-tests, Cohen's d, bootstrap intervals, and correlations

evaluationPage.title: The Evaluations Page
evaluationPage.description: Navigate to Evaluations in the sidebar for a cross-project view. Filter by project, model, metric, evaluation type, and annotator.

overview.title: Organizations
overview.description: Users must belong to at least one organization to access projects and data. Organizations are the primary unit for team collaboration and access control.
overview.note: If you see a warning about missing organization membership on the dashboard, contact your administrator to be added to an organization.

roles.title: Roles & Permissions
roles.description: BenGER uses a role-based access control system with four roles:

superadmin.title: Superadmin
superadmin.description: Full system access. Can manage all organizations, users, feature flags, and system settings.

orgAdmin.title: Org Admin
orgAdmin.description: Can manage organization members, invite new users, and configure organization settings.

contributor.title: Contributor
contributor.description: Can create and manage projects, import data, configure and run generations and evaluations.

annotator.title: Annotator
annotator.description: Can annotate tasks in projects they have been assigned to.

setup.title: Adding API Keys
setup.description: Navigate to your Profile page (click your name in the top-right corner). In the API Keys section, enter your keys for each LLM provider you want to use.
setup.step2: Scroll to the API Keys section
setup.step3: Enter the API key for each provider

providers.title: Supported Providers
providers.description: BenGER supports the following LLM providers. You need an API key from each provider you want to use:
providers.list: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepInfra, Mistral, Cohere, Grok, GLM

security.title: Security
security.description: API keys are encrypted before storage and are never exposed in the UI after saving. Each user manages their own keys independently.

noProjects.question: No projects visible
noProjects.description: The most common cause is that you are in the private ("Privat") context. Projects belong to organizations, so they are not visible in your private context.
noProjects.solution: Switch to the correct organization using the organization switcher in the sidebar. If you still don't see any projects, contact your administrator to be added to the organization.

generationFails.question: Generation fails or shows errors
generationFails.description: LLM generation tasks fail to complete.
generationFails.solution: Verify your API keys are entered correctly in your Profile. Check that the provider account has sufficient credits. Review the prompt structure for syntax errors (missing $ prefix, incorrect field names).

cannotAnnotate.question: Cannot annotate tasks
cannotAnnotate.description: The annotation interface is not accessible or tasks are not visible.
cannotAnnotate.solution: Check that your user role has annotation permissions. Annotators need to be assigned to the project. Contact your Org Admin if you need access.

evaluationEmpty.question: Evaluation shows no results
evaluationEmpty.description: Evaluation completes but no scores are displayed.
evaluationEmpty.solution: Ensure both human annotations and LLM generations exist for the tasks being evaluated. Check that the field mapping in the evaluation config correctly maps annotation fields to generation fields.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.description: Technical architecture and design of the BenGER project - Microservice architecture for evaluating Large Language Models in the legal domain.
architecture.subtitle: BenGER uses a modern **microservice architecture** consisting of several independent but collaborating components.

keyFeatures: Next.js 15 with App Router for modern routing and SSR
TypeScript for type safety and better developer experience
Tailwind CSS for responsive design and dark mode
Role-based access control and organization management

coreComponents: JWT-based authentication with role-based access control
Multi-organization support with flexible user management
Task management and data upload/synthesis capabilities
LLM integration with multiple providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepInfra, Mistral, Cohere, Grok, GLM)
Native annotation system for advanced annotation workflows
Real-time notification system with email support

workerTasks: Asynchronous LLM generation across multiple providers
Evaluation metric computation and statistical analysis

nativeAnnotation.title: Native Annotation System
nativeAnnotation.description: BenGER features a comprehensive, self-contained annotation system aligned with the Label Studio standard. This native solution eliminates external dependencies while providing collaboration features, real-time updates, and quality control mechanisms.
coreFeatures: Label Studio XML compatibility: Full support for Label Studio labeling configurations
Quality control: Inter-annotator agreement metrics and review workflows
Flexible project types: Question answering, multiple choice, exam solving, span annotation, and custom configurations
Rich annotation tools: Text highlighting, keyboard shortcuts, skip functionality, and annotation timer
Multiple export formats: JSON, CSV, XML, TSV, COCO, and CoNLL support
Annotation history: Track changes and review previous annotations
Role-based access: Annotator, contributor, and admin permissions with organization context
performanceImprovements: No external annotation service dependency - fully integrated into the platform
Server-Sent Events (SSE) for real-time notification delivery
WebSocket connections for live generation progress tracking
Configurable annotation assignments per task with review workflows

featureFlags.title: Feature Flag System
featureFlags.description: BenGER includes a feature flag system for controlling access to platform sections. Flags are binary on/off toggles managed by superadmins, enabling safe deployment and staged activation of new features.
keyCapabilities: Binary on/off toggles: Simple enable/disable for platform features
Superadmin management: Flag control via the /admin/feature-flags interface
Navigation integration: Flags control sidebar visibility of platform sections
Database-backed: Flag state persisted in PostgreSQL for consistency across restarts
usagePatterns: Flags are developer-defined and initialized on first setup

coreFunctions: Multi-membership: Users can belong to multiple organizations simultaneously
Subdomain routing: Each organization gets its own subdomain (org-slug.domain)
Role-based permissions: Org Admin, Contributor, and Annotator roles per organization
Invitation system: Email-based invitations for adding new members
Data isolation: Projects and data scoped to organization context
Shared projects: Projects can be shared across organizations

notificationTypes: Project lifecycle: Created, updated, shared, completed, archived, published
Data management: Import success/failure, export and upload completion
Annotation: Assignment, completion, batch completion, config updates
Task assignment: Assigned, removed, due soon, overdue
LLM generation and evaluation: Completion and failure notifications
System: Alerts, maintenance, security, API quota warnings

databases.postgresqlDesc: Primary data store for all application data, using Alembic for migrations.
postgresqlFeatures: JSONB fields for flexible metadata storage
databases.redisDesc: High-performance cache and message broker for real-time features.
redisFeatures: Task queue management for Celery workers
Temporary data and computation results cache

deployment.developmentDesc: Local development uses Docker Compose with hot-reloading for rapid iteration.
developmentFeatures: Automatic code reloading on changes via Turbopack
Traefik reverse proxy for subdomain routing (benger.localhost)
Isolated test infrastructure on separate ports (PostgreSQL 5433, Redis 6380)
Docker Compose profiles for development and testing
deployment.productionDesc: Production deployment on Kubernetes (k3s) with high availability and monitoring.
productionFeatures: Horizontal Pod Autoscaling based on CPU utilization
GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with automated testing

about.title: About BenGER
about.description: Learn more about BenGER, a framework for evaluating Large Language Models (LLMs) in the legal domain, developed at the Technical University of Munich (TUM).
about.subtitle: **BenGER (BENchmark for GERman Law)** is a research project by the Technical University of Munich in the field of Legal Technology. We investigate the suitability of Large Language Models (LLMs) for legal activities.
about.description2: One of the core projects is the creation of a holistic benchmark: Under the name BenGER (BENchmark for GERman Law), we are first building a framework in which such a benchmark can be realized, and then incrementally filling it with content in cooperation with other universities, companies and NGOs.

architecture.title: System Architecture
architecture.subtitle: **BenGER** is a comprehensive evaluation framework for Large Language Models in the German legal domain. It features a complete native annotation system with real-time collaboration, advanced quality control, and seamless workflow management.

content.frontendDesc: Modern **React-based frontend** with Next.js 15 App Router, providing a responsive and intuitive user interface for annotation tasks, evaluation management, and administrative functions.
content.apiGatewayDesc: **FastAPI-based backend** serving as the central API gateway, handling authentication, data processing, LLM integrations, and coordinating with worker processes for asynchronous tasks.
content.celeryWorkerDesc: **Distributed task processing** system using Celery workers for handling computationally intensive operations like LLM response generation, evaluation processing, and data synthesis.
content.nativeAnnotationDesc: **Comprehensive, self-contained annotation system** aligned with the Label Studio standard, eliminating external dependencies while providing collaboration features, real-time updates, and quality control mechanisms.
content.featureFlagsDesc: **Binary feature flag system** for controlling access to platform sections. Flags are simple on/off toggles managed by superadmins via the admin interface.
content.githubWorkflowDesc: **Automated GitHub Issue Workflow** system that ensures every issue is handled with production-ready quality standards through a comprehensive 5-step process from analysis to closure.
content.multiOrgDesc: **Multi-organization system** supporting collaborative research with subdomain-based routing, role-based permissions, data isolation, and flexible user management across organizations.
content.notificationDesc: **Real-time notification system** providing both in-app and email notifications for task lifecycle events, system alerts, organization activities, and long-running operations.
content.taskSystemDesc: **Universal Template System** for all task types with flexible JSON format support, backward compatibility, native annotation system integration, and round-trip export/import capabilities.
content.performanceDesc: **Comprehensive performance optimizations** resolving API timeout and stability issues through optimized database queries, Redis caching, query performance monitoring, and hot reload optimizations.
content.databasesDesc: **PostgreSQL as the primary database** with extended schema for multi-organization support, and **Redis for caching, task queues, and rate limiting** to ensure optimal performance.
content.deploymentDesc: **Kubernetes-based production deployment** with automated CI/CD pipelines, monitoring, and **local development environment** optimized for rapid iteration and testing.
